    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "pkg/registry/authorization/subjectaccessreview/rest.go", "symbol": "REST"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "pkg/registry/authorization/subjectaccessreview/rest.go", "symbol": "Create"},

    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "pkg/registry/rbac/rolebinding/policybased/storage.go", "symbol": "Storage"},

  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "pkg/kubeapiserver/authorizer/config.go", "symbol": "New"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go", "symbol": "RBACAuthorizer"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go", "symbol": "Authorize"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go", "symbol": "DefaultRuleResolver"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go", "symbol": "VisitRulesFor"}
  ],
  "text": "Kubernetes RBAC authorization is implemented through a comprehensive system of components working together to enforce role-based access control:\n\n## Core Authorization Component\nThe RBACAuthorizer (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go) is the central authorizer implementing the authorizer.Authorizer interface. It contains an authorizingVisitor that evaluates authorization requests by visiting rules from its RequestToRuleMapper. The Authorize method returns DecisionAllow/DecisionNoOpinion based on whether any rule matches the request.\n\n## Rule Resolution System\nThe DefaultRuleResolver (pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go) is the primary rule resolver that implements AuthorizationRuleResolver interface. It resolves policy rules for a given user in a namespace by:\n1. Listing all ClusterRoleBindings and RoleBindings that apply to the user\n2. Visiting each applicable role/clusterrole to collect its PolicyRules\n3. Using a ruleAccumulator visitor pattern to gather rules\n4. Implementing GetRoleReferenceRules to resolve Role/ClusterRole references\n\nThe resolver depends on four interfaces: RoleGetter, RoleBindingLister, ClusterRoleGetter, and ClusterRoleBindingLister for accessing the role-binding objects.\n\n## Subject Access Evaluation\nThe SubjectAccessEvaluator (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/subject_locator.go) evaluates which subjects (users, groups, service accounts) are allowed specific actions, used by authorization APIs to show what subjects can perform an action.\n\n## Subject Access Review REST Handler\nThe SubjectAccessReview REST handler (pkg/registry/authorization/subjectaccessreview/rest.go) implements the Create method to evaluate authorization requests. It uses the authorizer to make decisions and returns SubjectAccessReviewStatus with allowed/denied flags. The authorization REST storage provider (pkg/registry/authorization/rest/storage_authorization.go) registers all authorization endpoints including subjectaccessreviews, selfsubjectaccessreviews, localsubjectaccessreviews, and selfsubjectrulesreviews.\n\n## Bootstrap RBAC Policies\nBootstrap policies are defined in plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/:\n- policy.go: Defines cluster-wide roles and clusterrolebindings (ClusterRoles function)\n- namespace_policy.go: Defines namespace-scoped roles and rolebindings\n- controller_policy.go: Defines controller-specific roles and bindings\nThese are reconciled at startup via EnsureRBACPolicy hook in pkg/registry/rbac/rest/storage_rbac.go.\n\n## Escalation Prevention\nEscalation prevention is enforced through:\n1. EscalationAllowed (pkg/registry/rbac/escalation_check.go): Checks if user is in system:masters group\n2. RoleEscalationAuthorized: Checks if user has explicit \"escalate\" permission on roles/clusterroles\n3. BindingAuthorized: Checks if user has \"bind\" permission for role references\n\n## Policy-Based Storage Implementation\nPolicy-based storage wrappers prevent privilege escalation when creating/updating RBAC objects:\n- pkg/registry/rbac/role/policybased/storage.go: Role storage that enforces escalation checks\n- pkg/registry/rbac/rolebinding/policybased/storage.go: RoleBinding storage with binding authorization\n- pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrole/policybased/storage.go: ClusterRole storage with escalation prevention\n- pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrolebinding/policybased/storage.go: ClusterRoleBinding storage with escalation prevention\n\nThese storage implementations wrap standard storage and call EscalationAllowed or RoleEscalationAuthorized before allowing Create/Update operations.\n\n## Registry and Adapter Pattern\nEach RBAC resource type has:\n- Registry (role/registry.go, rolebinding/registry.go, etc.): Wraps storage with type information\n- AuthorizerAdapter: Adapts registries to the authorization validation interfaces\n- Strategy: Implements validation logic for resource operations\n\n## RBAC REST Storage Provider\nThe RESTStorageProvider (pkg/registry/rbac/rest/storage_rbac.go) orchestrates the entire RBAC subsystem by creating policy-based storage wrappers around standard storage and registering them for roles, rolebindings, clusterroles, and clusterrolebindings. It also provides the EnsureRBACPolicy post-start hook for bootstrapping default policies."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-client-go` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-client-go$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-api` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-api$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

**Sourcegraph Repositories:** `github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes`, `github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-client-go`, `github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-api`, `github.com/sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd`

# Kubernetes RBAC Authorization Implementation

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement RBAC authorization. Identify: the RBACAuthorizer with its Authorize method (in plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/), the SubjectAccessEvaluator, the DefaultRuleResolver that resolves PolicyRules for a user, the SubjectAccessReview REST handler, the bootstrap RBAC policy definitions, and the policy-based storage for Role and ClusterRoleBinding objects with escalation prevention.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you identify all the Go source files implementing RBAC authorization in the Kubernetes codebase. Let me start by searching for the key components.
Now let me create a comprehensive answer by compiling all the findings:

Perfect! I've identified all the Go source files implementing RBAC authorization in the Kubernetes codebase. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Components Identified:

### 1. **RBACAuthorizer** (`plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go`)
- Core authorizer with `Authorize` method
- Uses `authorizingVisitor` pattern to evaluate rules
- Works with `RequestToRuleMapper` interface

### 2. **SubjectAccessEvaluator** (`plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/subject_locator.go`)
- Evaluates which subjects can perform specific actions
- Used by authorization review APIs

### 3. **DefaultRuleResolver** (`pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go`)
- Implements `AuthorizationRuleResolver` interface
- Resolves PolicyRules for users by visiting RoleBindings/ClusterRoleBindings
- Uses `ruleAccumulator` visitor pattern
- Depends on RoleGetter, RoleBindingLister, ClusterRoleGetter, ClusterRoleBindingLister

### 4. **SubjectAccessReview REST Handler** (`pkg/registry/authorization/subjectaccessreview/rest.go`)
- Implements authorization review REST API
- Evaluates requests and returns allowed/denied status
- Registered via authorization REST storage provider (`pkg/registry/authorization/rest/storage_authorization.go`)

### 5. **Bootstrap RBAC Policies** (`plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/`)
- `policy.go`: Cluster roles and bindings
- `namespace_policy.go`: Namespace-scoped roles
- `controller_policy.go`: Controller-specific roles
- Reconciled at startup

### 6. **Policy-Based Storage with Escalation Prevention**
- `pkg/registry/rbac/role/policybased/storage.go`
- `pkg/registry/rbac/rolebinding/policybased/storage.go`
- `pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrole/policybased/storage.go`
- `pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrolebinding/policybased/storage.go`
- Enforces `EscalationAllowed` and `RoleEscalationAuthorized` checks

### 7. **Escalation Checks** (`pkg/registry/rbac/escalation_check.go`)
- `EscalationAllowed`: Checks for system:masters membership
- `RoleEscalationAuthorized`: Checks explicit "escalate" permission
- `BindingAuthorized`: Checks "bind" permission

The answer.json file at `/workspace/answer.json` contains the complete list of 27 files and 25 key symbols involved in RBAC authorization, along with a detailed narrative explaining how they work together.

    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes-kubernetes", "path": "pkg/kubeapiserver/authorizer/config.go", "symbol": "New"},

  "text": "Kubernetes RBAC authorization is implemented through a comprehensive system of components working together to enforce role-based access control:\n\n## Core Authorization Component\nThe RBACAuthorizer (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/rbac.go) is the central authorizer implementing the authorizer.Authorizer interface. It contains an authorizingVisitor that evaluates authorization requests by visiting rules from its RequestToRuleMapper. The Authorize method returns DecisionAllow/DecisionNoOpinion based on whether any rule matches the request.\n\n## Rule Resolution System\nThe DefaultRuleResolver (pkg/registry/rbac/validation/rule.go) is the primary rule resolver that implements AuthorizationRuleResolver interface. It resolves policy rules for a given user in a namespace by:\n1. Listing all ClusterRoleBindings and RoleBindings that apply to the user\n2. Visiting each applicable role/clusterrole to collect its PolicyRules\n3. Using a ruleAccumulator visitor pattern to gather rules\n4. Implementing GetRoleReferenceRules to resolve Role/ClusterRole references\n\nThe resolver depends on four interfaces: RoleGetter, RoleBindingLister, ClusterRoleGetter, and ClusterRoleBindingLister for accessing the role-binding objects.\n\n## Subject Access Evaluation\nThe SubjectAccessEvaluator (plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/subject_locator.go) evaluates which subjects (users, groups, service accounts) are allowed specific actions, used by authorization APIs to show what subjects can perform an action.\n\n## Subject Access Review REST Handler\nThe SubjectAccessReview REST handler (pkg/registry/authorization/subjectaccessreview/rest.go) implements the Create method to evaluate authorization requests. It uses the authorizer to make decisions and returns SubjectAccessReviewStatus with allowed/denied flags. The authorization REST storage provider (pkg/registry/authorization/rest/storage_authorization.go) registers all authorization endpoints including subjectaccessreviews, selfsubjectaccessreviews, localsubjectaccessreviews, and selfsubjectrulesreviews.\n\n## Bootstrap RBAC Policies\nBootstrap policies are defined in plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy/:\n- policy.go: Defines cluster-wide roles and clusterrolebindings (ClusterRoles function)\n- namespace_policy.go: Defines namespace-scoped roles and rolebindings\n- controller_policy.go: Defines controller-specific roles and bindings\nThese are reconciled at startup via EnsureRBACPolicy hook in pkg/registry/rbac/rest/storage_rbac.go.\n\n## Escalation Prevention\nEscalation prevention is enforced through:\n1. EscalationAllowed (pkg/registry/rbac/escalation_check.go): Checks if user is in system:masters group\n2. RoleEscalationAuthorized: Checks if user has explicit \"escalate\" permission on roles/clusterroles\n3. BindingAuthorized: Checks if user has \"bind\" permission for role references\n\n## Policy-Based Storage Implementation\nPolicy-based storage wrappers prevent privilege escalation when creating/updating RBAC objects:\n- pkg/registry/rbac/role/policybased/storage.go: Role storage that enforces escalation checks\n- pkg/registry/rbac/rolebinding/policybased/storage.go: RoleBinding storage with binding authorization\n- pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrole/policybased/storage.go: ClusterRole storage with escalation prevention\n- pkg/registry/rbac/clusterrolebinding/policybased/storage.go: ClusterRoleBinding storage with escalation prevention\n\nThese storage implementations wrap standard storage and call EscalationAllowed or RoleEscalationAuthorized before allowing Create/Update operations.\n\n## Registry and Adapter Pattern\nEach RBAC resource type has:\n- Registry (role/registry.go, rolebinding/registry.go, etc.): Wraps storage with type information\n- AuthorizerAdapter: Adapts registries to the authorization validation interfaces\n- Strategy: Implements validation logic for resource operations\n\n## RBAC REST Storage Provider\nThe RESTStorageProvider (pkg/registry/rbac/rest/storage_rbac.go) orchestrates the entire RBAC subsystem by creating policy-based storage wrappers around standard storage and registering them for roles, rolebindings, clusterroles, and clusterrolebindings. It also provides the EnsureRBACPolicy post-start hook for bootstrapping default policies."
}
